message:
fix(packs/Pack::check): segment-split compound commands at the pack level too

The previous commit taught `PackRegistry::check_command` to split
compound commands on `;` / `&&` / `||` / `\n`, but a single
`Pack::check(cmd)` call (used directly in the per-pack compound-command
regression test, and in any caller that walks packs individually)
still only checked the raw command. That meant the `docker ps; docker
system prune -a --volumes` regression test was passing only because
of the registry-level split — calling `packs::containers::docker::
create_pack().check(...)` directly would miss the destructive suffix.

Split inside `Pack::check` with the same `split_command_segments`
helper:

- Run `check_single(seg)` on each segment; the first destructive
  match blocks the compound command.
- Fall through to one more `check_single(cmd)` on the whole input so
  patterns that legitimately span segment boundaries (heredoc-aware,
  multi-segment pipeline patterns) still fire.

Extract the original checking logic into `check_single` so the
existing whole-command path stays a single no-branch call when the
input has no separators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
